mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 2.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month employment growth measured by PAYEMS exceeding 150,000 persons signals robust labor market strength that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained employment gains
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong aggregate demand that boosts corporate revenues and profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month by at least 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492,008 million and HOUST at 1,236 thousand as of 2023-08-15
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or interaction effect (measured as coefficient on RSAFS×HOUST interaction term) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 106.0 signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth and inflation expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 106.0 or declines month-over-month by more than 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerating above 250.0 combined with elevated federal funds rate above 4.5% signals persistent monetary tightening that constrains equity valuations and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 249.462 and FEDFUNDS at 1.51% as of 2023-09-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL×FEDFUNDS) fails to improve model fit (R² increase < 0.02) over single-variable models in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% or increases by more than 0.3 percentage points in one month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession phase
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured at quarterly frequency above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports equity valuations and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,286.497 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2023-04-15
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth quarters (GDPC1 > 2.5% annualized) are not significantly higher than low-growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,200 thousand units combined with falling retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,200 AND RSAFS declines month-over-month by more than 1%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,236 thousand and RSAFS at 492,008 million as of 2023-08-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 53% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (HOUST×RSAFS) fails to improve predictive power (Rank IC increase < 0.03) over single-variable models in any 2-year rolling window.
